questions.question: Describe the three main components of a centralized file system and how they differ from a distributed file system. (5 marks)
questions.hint: Centralized file systems, such as NFS, have file servers that manage access to files. In contrast, distributed file systems, such as DFS, use a network of nodes to store and manage files.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a file system namespace and a file system topology. (5 marks)
questions.hint: A file system namespace refers to the hierarchical structure of files and directories, whereas a file system topology refers to the physical distribution of files across a network of nodes.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the file service architecture in a distributed file system, and explain its benefits? (10 marks)
questions.hint: The primary function of the file service architecture is to provide a unified interface for accessing and managing files across a distributed network of nodes. The benefits of this architecture include improved scalability, fault tolerance, and performance.
